expert_rss.title: Violent Threats and Data Center Resistance Accelerate
expert_rss.summary: Americans hold an increasingly negative view of artificial intelligence (AI) and the vast infrastructure supporting its rapid growth and integration into the global economy. In November, The Soufan Center warned in an IntelBrief that violent expressions of anti-AI sentiment, particularly regarding data centers, were percolating online. Subsequent online surveying indicates that this deteriorating

expert_rss.summary:

expert_rss.summary:
Executive Summary: On April 25, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy visited Azerbaijan and held substantive talks with Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev in the northern city of Gabala (President of Ukraine, April 25). The meeting was the seventh between the two leaders in more than four years, but it was the first to produce a package of […]

expert_rss.title: Kremlin Uses Corruption Charges to Militarize Frontline Regions
expert_rss.summary: Executive Summary: Since 2025, Russian prosecutors have systematically brought criminal charges against governors and senior officials in Russia’s frontline regions: Kursk, Belgorod, and Bryansk. These charges have been for embezzlement and kickbacks in fortification projects. expert_rss.summary:
Executive Summary: On April 29, Russian President Vladimir Putin spoke to U.S. President Donald Trump in a 90-minute conversation that could have provided an opportunity to revive stalled peace negotiations with Ukraine (RIA Novosti, April 29). Instead, Putin opted to reiterate Russia’s territorial claims to Donbas, which, in Russian interpretation, are in line with the […]

expert_rss.title: 80 Years After Death, Medal of Honor Recipient Identified, Buried
expert_rss.summary: More than 80 years after his death, Medal of Honor recipient Army Capt. Willibald Bianchi's remains were identified by the Defense POW/MIA Accounting Agency, he was buried in his hometown alongside his parents and his sisters.

expert_rss.title: West Virginia Guardsmen Awarded Soldier, Airman Medals for Heroism in Nation's Capital
expert_rss.summary: Two West Virginia National Guardsmen were recognized in the Pentagon's Hall of Heroes for actions late last year while working as part of Joint Task Force District of Columbia — an effort directed by President Donald J. Trump to stop crime in the nation's capital and to beautify the city.

expert_rss.title: Project Flytrap 5.0 Puts Emerging Tech in Warfighters' Hands
expert_rss.summary: U.S. infantry soldiers and U.K. paratroopers prepared for the force-on-force phase of Project Flytrap 5.0, an exercise that integrates autonomous and unmanned ground vehicles, first-person view drones and counter-unmanned aerial systems on a simulated battlefield in Europe.

expert_rss.title: Eerie Company: A New Threat at the Joint Multinational Readiness Center
expert_rss.summary: Soldiers assigned to the newly established Eerie Company, 1st Battalion, 4th Infantry Regiment, used first-person-view drone systems to enhance reconnaissance capabilities during Joint Multinational Readiness Center exercises in Hohenfels, Germany.

expert_rss.title: Exercise Desert Hammer Prepares Citizen Airmen for 'Trial by Fire'
expert_rss.summary: Airmen assigned to the 944th Fighter Wing participated in Exercise Desert Hammer 26-2, training designed to build battlefield skills, test mission-essential tasks and prepare reserve airmen for operations beyond base, at Camp Navajo, Ariz.

expert_rss.title: Letter From The Editor: 10 Years And 11,000 Articles Later
expert_rss.summary:
TWZ was launched 10 years ago today and we are still here and growing thanks to all of you.
The post Letter From The Editor: 10 Years And 11,000 Articles Later appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: U.S. AH-64 Apache, MH-60 Seahawk Helicopters Sink Six Iranian Boats (Updated)
expert_rss.summary:
Commercial vessels and American warships are coming under Iranian attack around the Strait of Hormuz as a new U.S. maritime protection plan has kicked off.
The post U.S. AH-64 Apache, MH-60 Seahawk Helicopters Sink Six Iranian Boats (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Israel’s First KC-46 Tanker Is Now Flying
expert_rss.summary:
The first new tanker is set to arrive in Israel in around a month and will help relieve pressure on the IAF's tiny and very old KC-707 tanker force.
The post Israel’s First KC-46 Tanker Is Now Flying appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: First Ships Transit Strait Of Hormuz Under New U.S. Protection Plan
expert_rss.summary:
Project Freedom appears to be aimed, at least for now, at evacuating ships stuck in the Persian Gulf since the war with Iran began.
The post First Ships Transit Strait Of Hormuz Under New U.S. Protection Plan appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Confusion Surrounds Future Of U.S. Long-Range Missiles In Germany Amid Rift With White House
expert_rss.summary:
The latest U.S.-Germany rift could leave NATO in Europe outmatched by Russia’s long-range firepower, at least in the near term.
The post Confusion Surrounds Future Of U.S. Long-Range Missiles In Germany Amid Rift With White House appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: First B-52 To Arrive For Re-Engining At Boeing Plant Later This Year (Updated)
expert_rss.summary:
New Rolls-Royce F130 engines are a critical upgrade for Air Force B-52s, but work has been beset by delays and growing costs.
The post First B-52 To Arrive For Re-Engining At Boeing Plant Later This Year (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Where Are The Carriers As Of May 3, 2026: Ford Finally Heads Home
expert_rss.summary:
Two carrier strike groups remain in the Middle East as President Trump announces Project Freedom to get trapped ships out of the Persian Gulf.
The post Where Are The Carriers As Of May 3, 2026: Ford Finally Heads Home appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Israel To Buy Extra F-15IA And F-35I Squadrons
expert_rss.summary:
After years of high-tempo operations in the Middle East, Israel has locked in plans to further expand its airpower with new F-15IA and F-35I fighters.
The post Israel To Buy Extra F-15IA And F-35I Squadrons appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Could The X-BAT Stealth Fighter Drone Change The Air Combat Game?
expert_rss.summary:
We talk with Armor Harris, the chief designer of X-BAT, about its GE engine, thrust-vectoring controls, Hivemind AI 'pilot' and forthcoming flight-test plans.
The post Could The X-BAT Stealth Fighter Drone Change The Air Combat Game? appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Lawmakers push to expand gun rights on Army Corps of Engineers-run lands
expert_rss.summary: A group of GOP lawmakers is pressing the Army to loosen restrictions on firearms at recreation sites run by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers.

expert_rss.title: Air Force clears T-7A Red Hawk for low-rate production
expert_rss.summary: The decision authorizes a $219 million contract for the first 14 aircraft.

expert_rss.title: US military accompanies commercial carrier through Strait of Hormuz
expert_rss.summary: The shipping company Maersk said the transit of its Alliance Fairfax was completed without incident and that all crew were safe and unharmed.

expert_rss.title: National Guard officers earn medals for apprehending gunman in DC shooting
expert_rss.summary: Two West Virginia National Guard officers received medals for overpowering the gunman who shot two service members in November.

expert_rss.title: Career submariner selected to perform duties of under secretary of the Navy
expert_rss.summary: Retired Navy Capt. William Toti is filling in as the Navy’s No. 2 civilian.

expert_rss.title: US destroys six Iranian small boats, shoots down missiles, drones, admiral says
expert_rss.summary: Adm. Brad Cooper said Iran was trying to interfere with a U.S. military operation to open the Strait of Hormuz to commercial traffic.

expert_rss.title: Former Qatari aircraft on track for summer Air Force One delivery
expert_rss.summary: The new aircraft in the Boeing VC-25B Bridge program completed modifications and flight testing and is now being painting red, white and blue.

expert_rss.title: Impacts of Strait of Hormuz tensions stifle searches for US missing in Laos
expert_rss.summary: The ripple effects of the Iran war have spread to Laos, where fuel shortages have forced the cancellation or curtailment of searches by U.S recovery teams.

expert_rss.title: US denies Iranian reports of strike on warship, says ships transit Hormuz
expert_rss.summary: Two U.S.-flagged merchant ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM said on Monday as Iranian media circulated claims Iran struck a U.S. warship.

expert_rss.title: Interview: Gen. Ronald Clark, US Army Pacific commander
expert_rss.summary: For Clark, who has headed USARPAC since Nov. 8, 2024, this 41st iteration of Balikatan represents a “full-circle event.”

wikipedia.notes: Several perpetrators opened fire near the Israeli consulate building, Turkish police neutralized the attackers.
A man opened fire in a supermarket and took hostages before being killed by police. The Security Service of Ukraine said that it was investigating the attack as an act of terrorism.

An individual said to be linked to the terrorist group Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamia stabbed and injured two Jewish men in an antisemitic attack. The Metropolitan Police declared the incident an act of terrorism.
